name: Nine Jumps of Juneda Trail
name_original: La Ruta de la Banqueta — Los nueve saltos de Juneda
description: This easy 4.7-mile (7.6 km) hiking route in Juneda features the Nou Salts of the Canals d'Urgell and the Primera Máquina Hydroelectric Power
region_intro: Hiking around Juneda offers accessible natural features and serene landscapes, primarily characterized by its unique canal system. The terrain is generally flat or features gentle inclines, making it suitable for various fitness levels. Notable elements include tree-lined canal-side paths known as "Banqueta de Juneda" and the Salt del Durán waterfall. The region also features agricultural environments and nature reserves, providing a blend of natural beauty and local history. Best hiking trails around Juneda The most popular hiking route is Salto del Duran -- Pla d'Urgell, a 5.2 miles (8.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 8 minutes to complete, leading to the notable Salt del Durán waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Nine Jumps of Juneda Trail, an easy 4.7 miles (7.6 km) path. This route explores the local canal system and its unique features. Local hikers also love the Banqueta de Juneda loop from Juneda, a 3.5 miles (5.7 km) trail leading through tree-lined canal paths, often completed in about 1 hour 27 minutes. Hiking around Juneda is defined by tranquil canal-side paths, agricultural landscapes, and local natural features like the Salt del Durán waterfall. The network offers options for easy to moderate ability levels. The routes in Juneda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from around 8 reviews. More than 140 hikers have used komoot to explore Juneda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of hiking trails can I expect around Juneda?
A: Juneda's hiking trails are primarily characterized by accessible natural features and serene landscapes, often following the unique canal system. You'll find many tree-lined canal-side paths, known as the Banqueta de Juneda, offering tranquil walks. The terrain is generally flat or features gentle inclines, making it suitable for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for beginners or families in Juneda?
A: Yes, Juneda offers several easy routes perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938472264/nine-jumps-of-juneda-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nine Jumps of Juneda Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.7-mile (7.6 km) path exploring the local canal system. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19886337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Banqueta de Juneda loop from Juneda&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.5-mile (5.7 km) trail through pleasant canal paths.

Q: Can I find any circular hiking routes in the Juneda area?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19886337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Banqueta de Juneda loop from Juneda&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the distinctive tree-lined canal paths. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10466919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque del Terrall – Nou Salts of the Canals d'Urgell loop from les Borges Blanques&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 10.6 km trail.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on hikes near Juneda?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4591004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt del Durán Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural attraction in the area. You can visit it by hiking the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931116513/salto-del-duran-pla-d-urgell" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Duran -- Pla d'Urgell&lt;/a&gt; route, a moderate 5.2-mile (8.4 km) trail that leads directly to this picturesque 4.5-meter waterfall on the Urgell canal.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to explore while hiking around Juneda?
A: Beyond the canal paths, you can explore several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4591004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt del Durán Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a significant natural landmark. For a pleasant outdoor space, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5175602" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque del Terrall&lt;/a&gt; in nearby Les Borges Blanques, which offers green areas and a playground. The broader region also includes the Mas de Melons Nature Reserve, known for its rainfed agricultural environment and cultural sites like the Cogul cave paintings.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in the Juneda area?
A: There are over 25 hiking trails available around Juneda, offering a good variety for different preferences. Approximately half of these are rated as easy, while the other half are moderate, ensuring options for various fitness levels.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Juneda?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from around 8 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquil canal-side paths, the unique natural features like the Salt del Durán waterfall, and the generally accessible and pleasant rural scenery.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly hiking trails in Juneda?
A: Many of the canal-side paths and rural trails around Juneda are suitable for walking with dogs, provided they are kept under control. The generally flat terrain and open spaces make for enjoyable walks. Always ensure to follow local regulations regarding pets on trails.

Q: Are there any hiking routes that offer more challenging terrain or elevation gain near Juneda?
A: While Juneda itself is known for its gentle terrain, for more challenging hikes with significant elevation gain, you would need to venture into the wider Lleida province. This region offers access to the Catalan Pyrenees, with peaks reaching around 3,000 meters, providing rugged and high-mountain landscapes for experienced hikers.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads for hikes around Juneda?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For many routes starting directly from Juneda or nearby towns, you can often find street parking or designated parking areas within the villages. It's advisable to check specific route details on komoot for information on starting points and potential parking facilities.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Juneda?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the best times for hiking in Juneda. The weather is mild and pleasant, making for comfortable conditions on the trails. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if hiking during that season. Winters are typically mild, but some paths might be damp.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to a summit or offer panoramic views?
A: While Juneda's immediate vicinity is flatter, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14944046" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fita Alta Summit loop from Miralcamp&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.9 km trail that offers some elevation gain and the chance to reach a local high point, providing broader views of the surrounding agricultural landscapes.
tips: La Banqueta is the name given to the tree-lined paths that are on the sides of canals or rivers. Since the construction of the Canal de Urgell, thousands of trees have been planted in the main network of this canal with two purposes: to help stabilize the riverbed and to take advantage of the sidewalks, either with the production of fruits, firewood or wood from these trees.

In 1902, the small hydroelectric plant, known as the First Machine, was built. It took advantage of the waterfall for the generation of energy. The electricity produced served to bring light to the town of Juneda, being a heritage of special memory for its inhabitants.

At this point, we find nine waterfalls along 300 meters that cover a drop of 23 meters. An iconic place on the Juneda sidewalk route, especially at the time of the ditch, when the water falls with more force.
In 1911, a small hydroelectric power plant was built taking advantage of the almost 6 meter drop in height. With similar characteristics to the 1st Machine, it produced about 81 horses.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is an easy hiking trail, perfect for beginners, families, and hikers of all fitness levels. Its minimal elevation gain and gentle path make it a relaxing outing in nature.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 7.57 kilometers long and typically takes around 1 hour and 56 minutes to complete at a leisurely pace.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Route of the Bench?
faqs.answer: The terrain is generally flat and gentle, with a minimal elevation change of about 24 meters. You can expect easily accessible paths, typical of the Pla D'Urgell area, offering pleasant natural surroundings without significant technical challenges.

faqs.question: What interesting features or landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route features several points of interest, including the Banqueta de Juneda itself, which gives the trail its name and suggests benches for rest. You'll also pass by the Primera Máquina Hydroelectric Power Station, the impressive Nou Salts of the Canals d'Urgell, and the Waterfall of the Second Machine.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares sections with several other paths, including the Camí de la Primera Màquina, passeig de la Banqueta, Camí de la Segona Màquina, and parts of El Camí IT2TR2 El Camí de Ponent: de Balaguer a Montserrat, and Pedals del Canal d'Urgell.

faqs.question: Is the Route of the Bench — The nine jumps of Juneda dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs are not detailed, easy trails in natural areas are often dog-friendly. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash and ensure you clean up after them to maintain the natural environment for everyone.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its easy nature and location, the trail is enjoyable for most of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, while summer mornings or evenings can also be ideal to avoid the midday heat. Winter can be nice on clear days.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to hike this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access this trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details are not provided, as a popular local route, you can typically find parking in or near Juneda, close to the start of the trail. Look for designated parking areas within the town or along access roads.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities nearby?
faqs.answer: Juneda is a town, so you can expect to find amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and shops for refreshments or supplies before or after your hike. These are usually located within a short distance of the trail's access points.

faqs.question: What is the significance of the 'nine jumps' in the trail's name?
faqs.answer: The exact nature of the 'nine jumps' is not extensively detailed in available information, but it likely refers to a series of small cascades or steps along the canals or waterways that are part of the route, such as the Nou Salts of the Canals d'Urgell.

waypoints.name: Nou Salts of the Canals d'Urgell
